mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures aggregate consumer demand strength. A positive trend in RSAFS directly lifts corporate revenue expectations, causing S&P 500 forward returns to be positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth months are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) larger than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals tightening labor market conditions and rising household income expectations. This shifts aggregate demand expectations upward, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When triggered, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the triggered return distribution is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) creates a negative real interest rate environment. This environment forces capital out of fixed income and into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of joint-signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction during a monetary tightening cycle indicates that restrictive policy is suppressing real output. This suppresses aggregate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contraction episodes during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,000 thousand units indicate a durable recovery in residential construction and associated consumer durables demand. This level of activity translates into positive earnings revisions for cyclical sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances (HOUST > 1000) produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) provides a high-frequency signal of aggregate income formation. Positive payroll growth lifts near-term consumption and revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth months are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth above 2.5% annualized during the post-crisis recovery period signals that aggregate demand is absorbing slack without requiring premature monetary tightening. This macro environment supports equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over the post-crisis sample, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.25% reflects the zero lower bound policy regime. This extreme monetary accommodation suppresses short-term yields and induces portfolio rebalancing into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean excess return over cash is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).
